goal: Restore the repository working tree to the closest recoverable state from approximately three hours before the current local time, while preserving a safety snapshot of the current state first.
constraints: Determine the actual local current time before interpreting 'three hours ago'.
Create a safety snapshot before any destructive restore action.
Prefer Git-native recovery points from reflog/history over ad hoc file copying.
Do not permanently discard recoverable current work; preserve it in a named stash or equivalent backup first.
deliverables: A safety snapshot of the pre-restore state.
The repository restored to the nearest recoverable state from roughly three hours earlier.
A clear report of the exact timestamp and commit/reflog point used for restoration.
